Immediately after the Lakers were swept by the Oklahoma […]

articles.title: LeBron: 'I Don't Know What the Future Holds'
articles.original_title: LeBron: 'I don't know what the future holds'
articles.summary: LeBron James said he is undecided about his future after the Lakers were swept by the Oklahoma City Thunder in the second round, ending his record‑setting 23rd season with a 115‑110 loss in Game 4. The veteran star gave no indication whether he will stay in Los Angeles or explore other options.
key_points: Lakers eliminated in a 4‑game sweep by the Thunder.
LeBron finished a historic 23‑season career with the loss.
He remained noncommittal about playing next season in L.A. or elsewhere.

articles.title: French Big Men Face Off in Playoff Series
articles.original_title: The French Connection: Gobert, Wemby put their fri...
articles.summary: French forwards Rudy Gobert and Victor Wembanyama, longtime friends, met on opposite sides of a 2‑2 NBA playoff series. Their matchup highlights a decade‑long connection between the two players as they compete for their respective teams.
key_points: Gobert and Wembanyama have known each other for about ten years.
They are now opponents in a best‑of‑seven series tied at 2‑2.
The series showcases the growing impact of French talent in the NBA.

articles.title: OKC Escapes Late L.A. Charge to Complete Sweep
articles.original_title: OKC escapes late L.A. charge to complete sweep
articles.summary: The Oklahoma City Thunder held off a late comeback by the Los Angeles Lakers to finish a 4‑0 series sweep, improving to 8‑0 in the postseason. Despite a valiant effort from the Lakers, the Thunder secured the win and advanced.
key_points: Thunder completed a 4‑0 sweep of the Lakers.
Los Angeles fought back late but could not overcome the deficit.
Oklahoma City moved to an 8‑0 record in the 2024 playoffs.

articles.title: Mitchell's Record‑Tying 2nd‑Half 39 Points Even East Semis
articles.original_title: Mitchell's record-tying 2nd half evens East semis
articles.summary: Donovan Mitchell scored 39 points in the second half, tying an NBA playoff record, as the Cleveland Cavaliers beat the Detroit Pistons 112‑103 to even their second‑round series. The performance kept Cleveland alive in the Eastern Conference semifinals.
key_points: Mitchell's 39 second‑half points matched the playoff record.
Cavaliers won 112‑103, tying the series 2‑2 with Detroit.
The victory kept Cleveland's postseason hopes intact.

articles.title: Bickerstaff Calls Free‑Throw Disparity 'Unacceptable' After Loss
articles.original_title: Bickerstaff calls FT disparity in loss 'unacceptab...
articles.summary: Pistons head coach J.B. Bickerstaff criticized the large gap in free‑throw attempts between his team and the Cavaliers following Detroit's Game 4 defeat. He labeled the disparity as unacceptable and emphasized the need for better execution at the line.
key_points: Detroit attempted far fewer free throws than Cleveland in Game 4.
Coach Bickerstaff described the gap as unacceptable.
The free‑throw disparity contributed to the Pistons' loss.

articles.title: No discipline for Wemby elbow; he'll play Game 5
articles.original_title: No discipline for Wemby elbow; he'll play Game 5
articles.summary: Spurs star Victor Wembanyama will not face any further discipline for elbowing Minnesota's Naz Reid and will play in Game 5 against the Timberwolves in San Antonio, sources told ESPN.

articles.summary: The Spurs listed guard De'Aaron Fox as questionable for Tuesday's Game 5 of the Western Conference semifinals at Frost Bank Center because of right ankle soreness.

articles.title: All eyes on Wemby for Game 5 of Spurs-Timberwolves series, after his elbow merited Game 4 ejection
articles.original_title: All eyes on Wemby for Game 5 of Spurs-Timberwolves series, after his elbow merited Game 4 ejection
articles.summary: San Antonio's Victor Wembanyama is playing in Game 5 of the Spurs' Western Conference semifinal series against the Minnesota Timberwolves, after getting ejected early in Game 4 for throwing an elbow.  The Spurs are obviously relieved about that.  “I know he was upset not being able to play that game," Vassell said at a shootaround attended by Spurs President Gregg Popovich, Spurs legend Manu Ginobili and former Spurs assistant Brett Brown, among others.
